papers.title_en: Onsager--Machlup functionals for McKean--Vlasov SDEs via Euler-type approximation
papers.evidence: Onsager-Machlup泛函提供SDE的路径积分变分框架
papers.tldr: McKean-Vlasov随机微分方程的系数依赖于分布，导致其Onsager-Machlup泛函难以直接定义。本文引入基于经典无分布SDE的Euler型近似方案，通过逼近序列收敛论证，推导出显式的Onsager-Machlup泛函表达式。该构造性方法为分布依赖随机系统的最可能路径分析提供了新工具，并拓展了经典理论的应用范围。
papers.abstract_en: The Onsager--Machlup action functional provides a variational framework for characterizing the most probable transition paths of stochastic systems and plays an important role in the study of nonequilibrium fluctuations. Its extension to McKean--Vlasov stochastic differential equations is complicated by the intrinsic distribution dependence of the coefficients. In this paper, we address this difficulty by introducing an Euler-type approximation scheme based on classical, distribution-free stochastic differential equations. Combining the classical Onsager--Machlup theory with a convergence argument for the approximation sequence, we derive an explicit expression for the Onsager--Machlup functional associated with the McKean--Vlasov SDE. The proposed approach is constructive and extends to a broad class of distribution-dependent stochastic systems.

papers.title_en: Semimartingale Optimal Transport with Jumps: A General Framework and Equivalent Formulations
papers.evidence: 半鞅最优传输框架，处理边际分布和成本函数
papers.tldr: 研究带跳跃的半鞅最优传输（SOT）问题，成本依赖全微分特征。在成本函数仅需可测、凸和下半连续的最小假设下，证明最优计划存在并建立Kantorovich对偶，无需时间正则性。进一步揭示SOT的四种等价形式：Kantorovich对偶、HJB粘性解、Markovian投影鞅解、非局部FPK弱解。该框架统一推广经典最优传输、鞅最优传输、Schrödinger桥和重心弱最优传输，为带跳随机过程的最优控制提供基础。
papers.abstract_en: We study a semimartingale optimal transport (SOT) problem where the cost depends on the full differential characteristics, and the minimisation is over all semimartingale laws with given marginals whose absolutely continuous characteristics lie in a prescribed closed convex set. Under only minimal assumptions of measurability, convexity, and lower semicontinuity on the cost function, we prove the existence of an optimal plan for SOT and establish a Kantorovich-type duality without time-regularity conditions. We further prove that SOT admits four equivalent formulations: (i) a Kantorovich duality formulation, (ii) a viscosity solution formulation of the Hamilton--Jacobi--Bellman equation, (iii) a martingale solution formulation via Markovian projection, (iv) a PDE formulation via weak solutions of a non-local Fokker--Planck--Kolmogorov equation. The framework simultaneously generalises classical optimal transport, martingale optimal transport, Schrödinger bridge problems, and barycentric weak optimal transport.
